scenario: Gateway goes offline for 72 hours — nightly evolution runs fail — system performance degrades
failure_mode: Dissipative structure starvation: the intelligence IS the energy flow. Without nightly compute input, the ordered state (knowledge, weights, routes) decays toward thermal equilibrium (random).
lesson: Dissipative structures have a characteristic decay time. Measure it. Know how long the system can survive without energy input before structure degrades meaningfully.
counterfactual: If we knew the decay rate, we could trigger emergency evolution runs when gateway comes back online to restore structure from frozen history
prevention: Add gateway downtime tracking. On reconnect: run emergency KAIROS catch-up cycle scaled to downtime duration. Document expected decay rate from baseline.
